topic: Lunar 18.6-yr cycle via gears — c5 patch verification
our_argument_summary: WIN-020 attacks the dome's claim that the 18.613-year lunar nodal regression is produced by epicyclic gears, on three legs: (1) standard gravitational torque already predicts the value exactly; (2) the value is publicly known textbook physics, so the dome cannot claim it as a novel prediction; (3) the dome supplies no driver, tooth counts, or amplitude prediction. The verdict is Std Model Explains.

field_diffs.tldr_evidence: unchanged (284 chars) — NOT TOUCHED BY PATCHES
field_diffs.tldr_verdict: unchanged (155 chars) — NOT TOUCHED BY PATCHES
field_diffs.finding: unchanged (171 chars) — NOT TOUCHED BY PATCHES

c5_hole_disposition.assessment: The patch added the Saros distinction (Saros = ~18.03 yr, 223 synodic months) and the Clairaut 1749 attribution, and notes the 0.58-yr difference. Strong factual error is gone. Residual: the sentence still opens with "This cycle has been observed for over 2,000 years" where "This cycle" refers back to the 18.613-yr nodal regression named in the prior sentence — the em-dash continuation then admits ancient astronomers actually tracked the Saros. A pedantic defender can still note the referential mismatch. Severity downgraded from moderate to minor (now h0 below).

c5_hole_disposition.topic: at any precision at all phrasing
c5_hole_disposition.assessment: Decider removed the redundant emphatic "at all" (current: "...no testable deviation at any precision."). The c5 review also recommended inserting "as stated" but the decider went with the simpler deletion. The strongest part of the rhetorical vulnerability is gone; acceptable close.
Decider removed the second ... wrapper exactly as c5 recommendation option (a) specified. Paragraph 3 now has one bold sentence followed by a non-bold clarifying sentence. No residual.

Decider rewrote to "Modern lunar laser ranging (LLR) confirms lunar range to millimeter precision, constraining the gravitational-torque orbit model." This is more precise than c5's suggestion — lunar range is the actual LLR observable, and the constraint flows from there. The detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text formulations are now symmetric (both say range, not parameters).

Confirmed via byte-level scan: detail_verdict_text now contains 0 curly apostrophes (U+2019) and 4 straight ASCII apostrophes (0x27). All four are in "dome author's" / "dome's" constructions. Curly double-quotes (3 pairs) remain around "pending-refinement," "preliminary-result," "open-for-gear-ratio-derivation" — these predate the c5 patches and were not part of c5's flagged scope; flagging them now would be scope-creep.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No handler for WIN-020 in monitor.py. The 18.6-yr value appears in the WIN list as a metadata label only. No comparison, no fetch, no hardcoded expected value.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. detail_verdict_text correctly states "there is no monitor.py handler, no hardcoded expected value, and no live fetch; the period appears only as a metadata label."

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no domain/handler/fetch for the nodal cycle. Confirmed unchanged since c2.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The 18.613-yr nodal regression is derived from gravitational mechanics (Clairaut 1749). The dome relabels the cause as "epicyclic gears" while adopting the standard-physics numerical value.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The value is centuries old in published form (Clairaut 1749) and millennia old in the Saros-eclipse form (Antikythera ~100 BC). No git history shows a dome-side derivation; the period appears only as a label on a list of "confirmed" predictions.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation path exists from dome geometry (disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, sun_altitude=5733 km, moon_altitude=2534 km) to 18.613 yr. The dome's "gear ratio" phrasing is metaphorical with no tooth counts, driver, or rate equation.

holes_found.description: TLDR consistency: tldr_evidence still asserts "The 18.6-year lunar nodal cycle has been known for over 2,000 years — it's encoded in the Antikythera mechanism." This is exactly the Saros/nodal conflation the c5 hole #0 flagged in detail_evidence and that ISS-2195 fixed. The patch did not propagate to tldr_evidence. Per curmudgeon.md severity guide ("TLDR factual errors are major — readers see TLDRs first and may not expand"), this is a major hole. The argument-defending detail_evidence now correctly attributes the 2,000-yr observation to the Saros and credits Clairaut 1749 for the nodal derivation, but the casual reader who only reads the collapsed TLDR encounters the same false claim that motivated c5 hole #0 originally. A dome defender who chooses to attack our review will quote the TLDR, not the expanded detail.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch tldr_evidence: replace "The 18.6-year lunar nodal cycle has been known for over 2,000 years — it's encoded in the Antikythera mechanism." with something like "The 18.6-year lunar nodal regression was derived from gravitational mechanics by Clairaut in 1749. (Ancient astronomers tracked the related 18.03-year Saros eclipse cycle — encoded in the Antikythera mechanism — which is mathematically distinct.)" Adjust length as needed to stay TLDR-sized; the key swap is "known for over 2,000 years + Antikythera" → "derived by Clairaut 1749 + Saros distinction".

holes_found.description: Summary-table consistency: the finding column says "Non-discriminating: period is an input (known for over 2,000 years, encoded in Antikythera mechanism ~100 BC); dome's gears have no physical driver or amplitude prediction". Same Saros/nodal conflation — "period" here is the 18.6-yr nodal period from the claim cell, and the parenthetical asserts it was encoded in Antikythera, which the c5 review established (Freeth et al. 2006) is wrong: Antikythera tracks the Saros. Per curmudgeon.md ("A one-liner that overstates, understates, or mischaracterizes the detail evidence is a hole a defender can exploit — readers may never click through to the detail"), the summary-table row is even more high-traffic than the TLDR (it is the first surface anyone scanning the WIN index encounters).
holes_found.recommendation: Patch finding: replace "(known for over 2,000 years, encoded in Antikythera mechanism ~100 BC)" with "(value derived by Clairaut 1749; related Saros eclipse cycle known for >2,000 years)". Keep the rest of the sentence intact; the structural argument ("dome's gears have no physical driver or amplitude prediction") still stands.

holes_found.description: Residual on c5 hole #0 patch: in detail_evidence the sentence "Gravitational torque produces the exact 18.613-year nodal regression period. This cycle has been observed for over 2,000 years — ancient astronomers tracked the closely related Saros eclipse cycle..." has an awkward antecedent. "This cycle" grammatically refers to the 18.613-yr nodal regression named in the prior sentence, but the em-dash clause then says ancient astronomers tracked a DIFFERENT cycle (the Saros). A defender can pick at this: "the nodal cycle was NOT observed for 2,000 years — it was derived in 1749 and observationally refined since." The factual content downstream of the antecedent is now correct; only the connective is loose.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish — rewrite the connecting sentence: "Gravitational torque produces the exact 18.613-year nodal regression period. The closely related Saros eclipse cycle (~18.03 yr, 223 synodic months) has been observed for over 2,000 years — encoded in the Antikythera mechanism (~100 BC) and Babylonian records — and the two cycles differ by ~0.58 yr; the 18.613-yr nodal value itself was formally derived from gravitational mechanics by Clairaut (1749)." This puts the "observed for 2,000 years" claim on the Saros (where it is correct) and the "derived 1749" claim on the nodal value (where it is correct). Same length, no scientific change.

holes_found.description: tldr_verdict says "The value was publicly known for millennia." Applied strictly to the 18.613-yr nodal regression period, "millennia" is wrong — Clairaut derived it in 1749, so ~275 years, not millennia. The Saros was known for millennia; the nodal was not. Same family of imprecision as the other TLDR/finding issues. Defender retort is the same: "the nodal period is not the Saros period."
holes_found.recommendation: If you are patching tldr_evidence anyway, also patch tldr_verdict: "The value was publicly known for millennia." → "The value has been textbook physics for centuries (Clairaut, 1749)." Two-word swap, same TLDR length budget, removes the residual conflation.

kernel_of_truth.description: The c5 patches DID land the substantive scientific fix in detail_evidence (wins.json:020.detail_evidence). The Saros/nodal distinction is now explicit with synodic-month precision, the Clairaut (1749) attribution is in, and the LLR phrasing is symmetrically corrected across both detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text. The decider also created successor ISS-2195 (monitor/decisions/decider-report-2026-05-25T14-30.json:patches_applied) to address the c5 unresolved_prior_cycle finding that ISS-1196 had been wrongly closed in April. This is exactly the right pattern — close-without-verify gets surfaced and remediated via successor ISS rather than buried.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The fix is incomplete. The same Saros/nodal conflation that the c5 review (monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-020.c5.json:holes_found[0]) called a moderate hole still appears in two parallel surfaces (wins.json:020.tldr_evidence and wins.json:020.finding) that receive MORE reader traffic than the patched field. A dome defender quoting our work will quote the TLDR or the summary table, not the expanded detail block. Until the fix propagates, the c5 hole #0 remediation is publicly only half-applied.

deeper_analysis: This is a textbook case of the surface-propagation gap. The decider self-applied 5 patches in commit 4e79507; all 5 hit the correct fields named by the c5 hole descriptions. But the c5 review described holes in terms of specific fields (detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text), and the decider faithfully patched those specific fields without checking parallel surfaces — TLDRs, summary-table cells — that carry the same prose pattern. The result: detail_evidence now correctly says "Antikythera tracks the Saros, not the nodal" while tldr_evidence and finding (the higher-traffic surfaces) still say "the nodal is encoded in Antikythera". A dome defender can now legitimately point out the inconsistency: "your own expanded text contradicts your own headline." This is worse than uniform error and worse than uniform truth — it suggests careless updating. The decider's patch discipline needs a propagation rule: when patching a Saros/nodal-class factual error in detail_evidence, scan tldr_evidence and finding for the same prose pattern and offer parallel patches in the same commit. (Recommendation to surface in summary_for_decider so the operator/tinker can decide whether to encode that rule globally.)

citations_verified: https://www.nature.com/articles/nature03397 (Antikythera mechanism, Nature 2006) — link is in detail_evidence and the c5 review correctly cited Freeth et al. 2006 as the source for Antikythera tracking the Saros
citations_unchecked: Clairaut (1749) — added in this patch round, not given a clickable citation. Standard textbook attribution; would be cosmetic to add a link, not required for accuracy.

summary_for_decider: WIN-020 c5 patches verified: all 5 holes substantively closed in the patched fields (detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text). However, two new MAJOR holes surfaced: the Saros/nodal fix did not propagate to tldr_evidence and finding (both still say the 18.6-yr nodal cycle is encoded in Antikythera, which is the same error c5 hole #0 flagged). One minor antecedent residual in the patched detail_evidence and one minor tldr_verdict residual ("known for millennia" — applies to Saros, not nodal). Recommended: 4 small patches (2 major, 2 minor) for c7 round, plus consider a decider-side propagation rule: when patching a Saros/nodal-class factual error in detail_evidence, scan tldr_evidence + finding for the same prose pattern in the same commit. ISS-1196 successor-ISS-2195 closure pattern is correct and should be preserved.
